text_en: Recalling further the United Nations Millennium Declaration, adopted by the General Assembly on 8 September 2000,1 welcoming the commitment expressed therein to take measures to put an end to the ever-increasing acts of racism and xenophobia in many societies and to promote greater harmony and tolerance in all societies, and looking forward to its effective implementation at all levels, including in the context of the Durban Declaration and Programme of Action adopted by the World Conference against Racism, Racial Discrimination, Xenophobia and Related Intolerance, held in Durban, South Africa, from 31 August to 8 September 2001, 2

text_en: Reaffirming that discrimination on the basis of religion or belief constitutes a violation of human rights and a disavowal of the principles of the Charter,

text_fr: Convaincue que le respect des diversitÃĐs culturelles, ethniques, religieuses et linguistiques, de mÃŠme que le dialogue entre les civilisations et au sein de celles-ci, sont indispensables pour la paix, la comprÃĐhension et l'amitiÃĐ entre les individus et les peuples appartenant aux diffÃĐrentes cultures et nations dans le monde, alors que les manifestations de prÃĐjugÃĐs culturels, d'intolÃĐrance et de xÃĐnophobie Ã  l'ÃĐgard de personnes appartenant Ã  des cultures, religions et convictions diffÃĐrentes engendrent la haine et la violence entre les peuples et les nations Ã  travers le monde,
text_en: Convinced that respect for cultural, ethnic, religious and linguistic diversity and dialogue among and within civilizations are essential for peace, understanding and friendship among individuals and peoples belonging to different cultures and nations throughout the world, while manifestations of cultural prejudice, intolerance and xenophobia towards persons belonging to different cultures, religions and beliefs generate hatred and violence among peoples and nations throughout the world,

text_en: Recognizing the valuable contributions made by all religions and beliefs to modern civilization, and recognizing that dialogue among civilizations can contribute to raising awareness and understanding of common values,

text_en: Reaffirming the need for all States to continue their national and international efforts to intensify dialogue and broaden understanding among civilizations, cultures, religions and beliefs, and stressing that States, regional organizations, non-governmental organizations, religious organizations and the media have an important role to play in the promotion of tolerance and respect for freedom of religion and belief,

text_en: Deeply alarmed by the increasing trend of discrimination on the basis of religion and belief, in particular as a result of certain national policies and laws that stigmatize groups of persons belonging to certain religions and beliefs under various pretexts related to security and illegal immigration,

text_en: Alarmed at the serious manifestations of intolerance, discrimination and violence based on religion or belief, as well as acts of intimidation and coercion motivated by extremism, religious or otherwise, which occur in many parts of the world, in addition to the negative image of Islam in the media and the adoption and implementation of laws that explicitly discriminate against and target Muslims, in particular Muslim minorities since the events of 11 September 2001, and threaten the enjoyment of human rights and fundamental freedoms,

text_en: Noting with concern that defamation of religions may lead to social discord and violations of human rights,

text_en: Recalling its resolution 61/164 of 19 December 2006,

4. Se dÃĐclare profondÃĐment prÃĐoccupÃĐe par les programmes et orientations qui, dÃĐfendus par des organisations et des groupes extrÃĐmistes, visent Ã  diffamer les religions et Ã  inciter Ã  la haine religieuse, en particulier quand ils sont tolÃĐrÃĐs par des gouvernements ;
5. Se dÃĐclare de mÃŠme profondÃĐment prÃĐoccupÃĐe par le fait que l'islam est souvent  et  faussement  associÃĐ  aux  violations  des  droits  de  l'homme  et  au terrorisme ;

DÃĐplore l'utilisation  de  la  presse  ÃĐcrite,  des  mÃĐdias  audiovisuels  et ÃĐlectroniques, y compris l'internet, et de tous autres moyens dans le but d'inciter Ã  des actes de violence, Ã  la xÃĐnophobie ou Ã  l'intolÃĐrance qui y est associÃĐe et Ã  la discrimination Ã  l'ÃĐgard de l'islam ou de toute autre religion, ainsi que les actes visant les symboles religieux ;
9. Souligne la  nÃĐcessitÃĐ  de  lutter  efficacement  contre  la  diffamation  de toutes les religions et l'incitation Ã  la haine religieuse, en particulier contre l'islam et les musulmans ;

10. Insiste sur le droit de chacun Ã  la libertÃĐ d'opinion sans restriction et Ã  la libertÃĐ  d'expression,  dont  l'exercice  s'accompagne  de  responsabilitÃĐs  et  devoirs spÃĐciaux et peut faire l'objet de restrictions prescrites par la loi et exigÃĐes par le respect des droits ou de la rÃĐputation d'autrui, la sÃĐcuritÃĐ nationale et la sÃŧretÃĐ publique,  la  santÃĐ  ou  la  morale  publique  et  le  respect  des  religions  et  des convictions ;

11. Exhorte les Ãtats Ã  prendre des mesures pour interdire la promotion de la haine  raciale,  religieuse  ou  fondÃĐe  sur  l'origine  nationale,  qui  constitue  une incitation Ã  la discrimination, Ã  l'hostilitÃĐ ou Ã  la violence ;
items_en: 1. Takes note of the report of the Secretary-General 5 and the conclusions contained therein;
2. Expresses its deep concern at negative stereotypes of religions and manifestations of intolerance and discrimination in respect of religion or belief still manifest throughout the world;
3. Strongly deplores the violence and violence against businesses, cultural centres and places of worship of all religions, as well as acts aimed at religious symbols;

4. Expresses deep concern at the programmes and orientations promoted by extremist organizations and groups aimed at defaming religions and inciting religious hatred, in particular when tolerated by Governments;
5. Also expresses its deep concern that Islam is often and falsely associated with human rights violations and terrorism;
6. Notes with deep concern that the campaign of defamation of religions and ethnic and religious stereotyping of Muslim minorities has intensified since the tragic events of 11 September 2001;

7. Recognizes that, in the context of the fight against terrorism and the response to counter-terrorism measures, defamation of religions and incitement to religious hatred become an aggravating factor that contributes to the denial of the fundamental rights and freedoms of members of target groups and to their economic and social exclusion;

8. Deplores the use of print, audio-visual and electronic media, including the Internet, and any other means for the purpose of inciting acts of violence, xenophobia or related intolerance and discrimination against Islam or any other religion, as well as acts aimed at religious symbols;
9. Stresses the need to effectively combat defamation of all religions and incitement to religious hatred, in particular against Islam and Muslims;

10. Stresses the right of everyone to freedom of opinion without restriction and to freedom of expression, the exercise of which is accompanied by special responsibilities and duties and may be subject to restrictions prescribed by law and required by respect for the rights or reputations of others, national security and public safety, public health or morals and respect for religions and beliefs;
11. Urges States to take measures to prohibit the promotion of racial, religious or national hatred, which constitutes incitement to discrimination, hostility or violence;

Engage vivement les Ãtats Ã  assurer Ã  tous, en droit et dans la pratique, l'ÃĐgalitÃĐ d'accÃĻs Ã  l'ÃĐducation, notamment l'accÃĻs de tous les enfants, filles comme garÃ§ons, Ã  l'enseignement primaire gratuit, et l'accÃĻs des adultes Ã  l'ÃĐducation et Ã  la  formation  permanentes  fondÃĐes  sur  le  respect  des  droits  de  l'homme,  de  la diversitÃĐ et de la tolÃĐrance, sans discrimination aucune, et Ã  s'abstenir de toutes mesures juridiques ou autres entraÃŪnant une sÃĐgrÃĐgation raciale dans l'accÃĻs Ã  la scolarisation ;

Demande Ã   la  communautÃĐ  internationale  de  favoriser  un  dialogue  Ã  l'ÃĐchelle mondiale en vue de promouvoir une culture de tolÃĐrance et de paix fondÃĐe sur  le  respect  des  droits  fondamentaux  et  de  la  diversitÃĐ  des  religions  et  des convictions, et prie instamment les Ãtats, les organisations non gouvernementales, les  organismes  religieux,  la  presse  et  les  mÃĐdias  ÃĐlectroniques  de  soutenir  ce dialogue et d'y participer ;

Affirme que le Conseil des droits de l'homme doit promouvoir le respect universel  de  toutes  les  valeurs  religieuses  et  culturelles  et  s'attaquer  aux  cas d'intolÃĐrance, de discrimination et d'incitation Ã  la haine Ã  l'encontre des membres de toute communautÃĐ ou des adeptes de toute religion ;

18. Prend note des efforts dÃĐployÃĐs par la Haut-Commissaire des Nations Unies aux droits de l'homme pour promouvoir les questions relatives aux droits de l'homme et les inclure dans les programmes ÃĐducatifs, en particulier le Programme mondial  d'ÃĐducation  dans  le  domaine  des  droits  de  l'homme  proclamÃĐ  par l'AssemblÃĐe gÃĐnÃĐrale le 10 dÃĐcembre 2004 6 et invite la Haut-Commissaire Ã  :
a ) Poursuivre  ces  efforts,  en  mettant  l'accent  sur  les  contributions qu'apportent les cultures ainsi que la diversitÃĐ religieuse et culturelle ;

b ) Collaborer avec d'autres organisations internationales compÃĐtentes Ã  la tenue de confÃĐrences communes visant Ã  encourager le dialogue entre civilisations et Ã  promouvoir la comprÃĐhension de l'universalitÃĐ des droits de l'homme et leur mise en Åuvre Ã  divers niveaux, en particulier avec le Bureau du Haut-ReprÃĐsentant
items_en: 12. Also urges States to provide, within their domestic legal and constitutional systems, adequate protection against acts of hatred, discrimination, intimidation and coercion resulting from defamation of religions, to take all possible measures to promote tolerance and respect for all religions and beliefs and to promote understanding of their value systems, and to complement their legal systems by combining intellectual and moral strategies to combat religious hatred and intolerance;

13. Calls upon all States to ensure that all State officials, law enforcement officials, military personnel, civil servants and teachers, in the performance of their official duties, respect people regardless of their different religions and beliefs and do not discriminate against anyone on the basis of religion or belief, and to ensure that they receive the necessary and appropriate education or training;

14. Stresses the need to combat defamation of religions and incitement to religious hatred by developing strategies and harmonizing actions at the local, national, regional and international levels through education and awareness-raising campaigns;

15. Urges States to ensure to all, in law and in practice, equal access to education, including access to free primary education for all children, both girls and boys, and access for adults to continuing education and training based on respect for human rights, diversity and tolerance, without discrimination of any kind, and to refrain from any legal or other measures leading to racial segregation in access to schooling;

16. Calls upon the international community to foster a global dialogue with a view to promoting a culture of tolerance and peace based on respect for human rights and the diversity of religions and beliefs, and urges States, non-governmental organizations, religious organizations, the press and the electronic media to support and participate in this dialogue;

17. Affirms that the Human Rights Council should promote universal respect for all religious and cultural values and address cases of intolerance, discrimination and incitement to hatred against members of any community or adherents of any religion;

18. Takes note of the efforts of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights to promote human rights issues and to include them in educational programmes, in particular the World Programme for Human Rights Education proclaimed by the General Assembly on 10 December 2004, 6 and invites the High Commissioner to:
( a ) Continue these efforts, focusing on the contributions of cultures and religious and cultural diversity;

(b) Collaborate with other relevant international organizations in the holding of joint conferences to encourage dialogue among civilizations and to promote understanding of the universality of human rights and their implementation at various levels, in particular with the Office of the High Representative

items_en: 19. Requests the Secretary-General to submit to the General Assembly at its sixty-third session a report on the implementation of the present resolution, including on the possible correlation between defamation of religions and the rise of incitement, intolerance and hatred in many parts of the world.
